sections.paragraph:
διασείω 1 aor. διέσεισα, aor. pass. ptc. διασεισθέντες 3 Macc 7:21 (s. σείω; Hdt. et al.; Philo, Leg. All. 2, 99; Jos., Bell. 3, 221 al.) extort money by force or threat of violence, extort (lit. ‘shake violently’; cp. our colloq. ‘shake down’) legal t.t. (UPZ 162 VIII, 13 [117 b.c.]; 192, 7; 193, 8; POxy 240, 5 [37 a.d.] διασεσεισμένῳ ὑπὸ στρατιώτου; 284, 5 [50 a.d.]; PTebt 43, 26 [118 b.c.] συκοφαντηθῶμεν διασεσεισμένοι; 3 Macc 7:21) w. acc. (UPZ 161, 37 [119 b.c.]; PAmh 81, 6) μηδένα from no one Lk 3:14.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

colloq. colloq. = colloquial

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

acc. acc. = accusative

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 236
